parsed_args.prompt: What related-party relationships exist within the consolidated structure following the xAI and X Holdings mergers? Where does the filing discuss pre-merger intercompany activity between SpaceX, xAI, and X Holdings? Look for sections on related party transactions, common control, intercompany activity, and pre-merger transactions. {{spacex_s1}}

tool_output.output:
# Related-Party Relationships and Pre-Merger Intercompany Activity

## I. Where the Filing Discusses Pre-Merger Intercompany Activity

The filing addresses pre-merger intercompany activity between SpaceX, xAI, and X Holdings in several distinct locations:

### A. "Certain Relationships and Related Person Transactions" Section

This section (governed by Item 404 of Regulation S-K) explicitly extends to pre-merger activity:

> "the transactions described below also include certain agreements and transactions originally entered into by xAI or X Holdings prior to the xAI Merger to the extent that such agreements and transactions are ongoing following the consummation of [the xAI Merger]."

It establishes a three-part threshold for disclosure: (1) the company is a participant; (2) the amount exceeds $120,000; and (3) a director, executive officer, 5%+ holder, or their immediate family member has a direct or indirect material interest.

### B. Note 1 — Nature of Business (Common Control Accounting)

The filing states that the Mergers were accounted for as **reorganizations of entities under common control** because Mr. Musk held a controlling financial interest in SpaceX, xAI, and X through his majority voting interest in each entity. Critically:

> "all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation."

This means that pre-merger transactions among SpaceX, xAI, and X themselves are **not** separately disclosed as related-party transactions in the consolidated financial statements—they are eliminated. What remains disclosed are transactions with **external** related parties (Tesla, The Boring Company, Valor, Musk-affiliated entities) that persist post-merger.

### C. Prospectus Preamble (Retrospective Recasting)

> "The summary historical consolidated financial and operating data of SpaceX has been prepared to reflect the retrospective combination of the companies for all periods presented to include the historical results of xAI... and X Holdings... because these transactions were between entities under common control."

The preamble also notes this presentation "constitutes a change in reporting entity."

---

## II. Related-Party Relationships Within the Consolidated Structure

### A. Internal Corporate Structure (Post-Merger)

| Entity | Relationship | Acquisition Date |
|---|---|---|
| **SpaceX** (parent) | Reporting company | — |
| **xAI (X.AI Holdings Corp.)** | Wholly-owned subsidiary of SpaceX | February 2, 2026 ("xAI Merger Date") |
| **X Holdings Corp. ("X")** | Wholly-owned subsidiary of xAI | March 28, 2025 ("X Merger") |
| **X.AI Corp.** | Wholly-owned subsidiary of xAI | March 28, 2025 |
| **Falcon Landing, LLC** | SpaceX subsidiary operating aircraft | — |
| **CTC** | Subsidiary of xAI / indirect subsidiary of SpaceX | — |

All historical intercompany transactions among these entities have been eliminated in consolidation.

---

## III. External Related-Party Transactions (Ongoing Post-Merger)

### A. Transactions with Elon Musk and Affiliated Entities

#### 1. Tesla, Inc.

Mr. Musk serves as CEO and director of Tesla and holds approximately 20% of Tesla stock. Tesla, in turn, holds 18,990,195 shares of SpaceX Class A common stock (<1.0% post-offering).

**SpaceX commercial, licensing, and support agreements with Tesla:**

| Period | Goods/Services Obtained |
|---|---|
| 2023 | $11 million |
| 2024 | $4 million |
| 2025 | $144 million |
| Jan 1–Feb 28, 2026 | $0.2 million |

**xAI commercial, licensing, and support agreements with Tesla:**

| Period | Goods/Services Obtained | Revenue Recognized by xAI |
|---|---|---|
| 2024 | $191 million | — |
| 2025 | $506 million | $2 million |
| Jan 1–Feb 28, 2026 | $34 million | $0.4 million |

**X Holdings advertising agreements (Tesla purchasing ads on X):**

| Period | Advertising Purchased |
|---|---|
| 2024 | $0.5 million |
| 2025 | $4 million |
| Jan 1–Feb 28, 2026 | $0 |

**Aircraft usage (SpaceX invoicing Tesla):**

Since April 2016, SpaceX has owned and operated aircraft used by Mr. Musk (in his capacity as Tesla CEO) and other Tesla personnel. Rates were determined by Tesla and SpaceX subject to FAA rules.

| Period | Amount Charged to Tesla |
|---|---|
| 2023 | $1 million |
| 2024 | $1 million |
| 2025 | $2 million |
| Jan 1–Feb 28, 2026 | $0 |

**Capital equipment purchases from Tesla:**

- Megapack products: $506 million (2025), $191 million (2024)—recorded in Property, plant, and equipment, net
- Cybertrucks: $131 million at MSRP (2025)—recorded in Property, plant, and equipment, net

#### 2. The Boring Company

- **Office lease:** In 2024, X entered into an office space lease with a Boring Company subsidiary. Lease payments: $0.1M (2024), $1M (2025), $0.1M (Jan–Feb 2026).
- **Tunnel construction:** SpaceX incurred $1 million in 2025 for tunnel construction by The Boring Company in Bastrop, Texas.

#### 3. Musk Industries LLC (real property lease to xAI)

xAI leases real property owned by Musk Industries LLC (owned by Mr. Musk). Lease payments: $0.5M (2024), $2M (2025), $0.2M (Jan–Feb 2026).

#### 4. Security Company (Musk-owned)

SpaceX is party to a services agreement with a security company owned by Mr. Musk, organized to provide security services concerning him, including in connection with his SpaceX duties.

| Period | SpaceX Security Expenses |
|---|---|
| 2023 | $2 million |
| 2024 | $3 million |
| 2025 | $4 million |
| Jan 1–Feb 28, 2026 | $1 million |

#### 5. Elon Musk Stock Purchases

In 2025, Mr. Musk, through his trust, purchased $1,421 million of common stock from current and former shareholders.

---

### B. Transactions with Antonio J. Gracias and Valor Equity Partners

Mr. Gracias serves on SpaceX's board and is the founder, CEO, and Chief Investment Officer of Valor Equity Partners.

#### 1. Computing Equipment Leases (xAI subsidiaries → Valor)

Three equipment lease agreements between a subsidiary of xAI (CTC) and Valor for computing and related equipment:

| Lease | Aggregate Cash Payments (Life of Lease) |
|---|---|
| Lease 1 | $6,986 million |
| Lease 2 | $6,633 million |
| Lease 3 | $6,587 million |
| **Total** | **$20,206 million** |

- **Guarantor:** Space Exploration Technologies Corp. (or one of its subsidiaries) guarantees the lessees' payment and performance obligations.
- **Payments made:** $885 million in 2025; $857 million from January 1–February 28, 2026.

#### 2. Failed Sale-Leaseback Accounting (Valor Transaction)

The Valor transaction was deemed a **failed sale-leaseback**, resulting in:
- Debt recorded: $455 million (current) + $4,052 million (long-term) = **~$4,507 million** as of December 31, 2025 (one chunk references $5,365 million total, potentially including a second "Valor transaction II")
- Interest expense: $66 million for the year ended December 31, 2025
- Related asset recorded within Property, plant, and equipment, net

#### 3. X API Services (Valor → X)

X received payments from Valor for API services: $1M (2024), $1M (2025), $0.1M (Jan–Feb 2026).

---

### C. xAI Merger Consideration (Related-Party Transaction)

On February 2, 2026, SpaceX issued to xAI stockholders:
- **321.7 million shares** of Class A Common Stock (pre-2026 Stock Split basis)
- **121.7 million shares** of Class B Common Stock (pre-2026 Stock Split basis)
- **$2,947 million in cash**

---

### D. Tesla's xAI Investment and SpaceX Class A Issuance

- **January 2026:** Tesla agreed to invest $2,000 million in xAI Series E Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock, conditioned on regulatory approvals.
- **Following xAI Merger:** Tesla's right to acquire xAI Series E converted into the right to acquire SpaceX Class A common stock.
- **March 12, 2026:** SpaceX issued 3.8 million shares of Class A Common Stock (pre-2026 Stock Split basis) to Tesla.

---

### E. Share Repurchases from Related Parties

| Period | Shares | Amount | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2024 | 38.7M SpaceX common | $920M | Eligible employees and existing shareholders |
| 2024 | 0.1M SpaceX preferred | $101M | Existing shareholders at fair market value |
| 2025 | 14.0M SpaceX common | $522M | Eligible current/former employees |
| 2025 | 11.8M xAI common | $600M | Existing xAI shareholder |
| Q1 2026 | 25.4M SpaceX common | $2,413M | Eligible current/former xAI employees (xAI Merger-related) |
| Q1 2026 | 30.5M SpaceX common + 2.1M preferred | $1,933M | Existing shareholders at fair market value |
| Q1 2025 | 28.0M SpaceX common | $508M | Eligible employees and existing shareholders |

All repurchased shares have been retired.

---

### F. Other Director and Executive Officer Transactions

SpaceX owns and operates, through Falcon Landing, LLC (a subsidiary), three aircraft for use by directors, executive officers, and other personnel.

---

## IV. Summary of Key Disclosure Locations

| Topic | Location in Filing |
|---|---|
| Common control accounting & elimination of intercompany transactions | Note 1 (Nature of Business); Prospectus preamble |
| Retrospective recasting of financial statements | Prospectus preamble (Selected Financial Data); Note 1 |
| Pre-merger agreements ongoing post-merger | "Certain Relationships and Related Person Transactions" section |
| Tesla commercial/advertising/aircraft transactions | "Transactions with Elon Musk and Affiliated Entities" |
| Boring Company, Musk Industries, security company | Same section, subsequent paragraphs |
| Valor equipment leases and failed sale-leaseback | "Relationship with Antonio J. Gracias and Affiliated Entities"; Note 10 (Debt) cross-reference |
| xAI Merger consideration | Note 13 (Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Shareholders' Equity) |
| Tesla xAI investment conversion | Separate subsection within related party disclosures |
| Share repurchase activity | Capital stock roll-forward tables; Note 12/13 |

The filing's architecture is such that **intra-group** pre-merger activity (SpaceX ↔ xAI ↔ X) is eliminated and not separately disclosed, while **external** related-party transactions that continue post-merger are aggregated and presented by counterparty (Tesla, Boring Company, Valor, Musk-affiliated entities) with the understanding that some originated at the xAI or X level before consolidation.
